hypothesis: Repeated XR rejection can be reduced if every `sysopt.xr-*` card pins whether flagship invariants are currently intact from the authoritative `/work/xr` gap artifact, so route-neutral system work cannot keep retargeting IMC isolation when XR fails elsewhere.
measurement: Before the ratchet, `node CLAW/scripts/verify-systems-optimizer.mjs` accepted `sysopt.xr-*` cards that bound the authoritative XR gap, first broken law, severity baseline, quality baseline, and handoff yet still omitted whether `GGD/gaps/routes/work-xr.geometry-gap.json.flagship_invariants_intact` remained `true`, so route-neutral system work could keep misdiagnosing shared-lane XR failure as IMC flagship drift. After the ratchet, every XR card must pin `flagship_invariants_baseline` to `target_gap.flagship_invariants_intact`, and the verifier rejects any missing or stale baseline while the fixed scope-local promotion bundle stays green.
keep_rule: Keep only if the verifier fails XR cards whose `flagship_invariants_baseline` is missing or drifts from `target_gap.flagship_invariants_intact`, and the fixed scope-local evaluation bundle passes without touching route implementation paths.
learning_item: XR systems-optimizer ratchets must pin whether flagship invariants are currently intact; evaluator focus alone still lets route-neutral work misdiagnose shared-lane XR failure as IMC isolation drift.
